name: Hanmo (Cynthia) Yang

subject.title: Medicaid_Expenditure_Projection_Model
details: Ingested and structured 13 years of historical Medicaid expenditure data (2013–2025) into a pandas DataFrame with annual and monthly granularity.
Generated a monthly interpolated time series (156 data points) using linear regression on fractional year values to support ARIMA and Holt-Winters modeling.
Performed exploratory data analysis (EDA): computed year-over-year growth rates and decomposed the monthly series into trend, seasonal, and residual components using statsmodels.
Built and evaluated four forecasting models — Linear Regression, Polynomial Regression (degree 2), ARIMA(1,1,1), and Holt-Winters with damped trend — using a train/test split of 10/3 years.
Compared models using MAE, RMSE, and MAPE metrics; implemented automatic best-model selection ranked by MAPE.
Generated a 10-year projection table (2026–2035) with point estimates and 95% confidence intervals, exported to CSV.
Produced five matplotlib visualizations: historical trend, YoY growth bar chart, all-model comparison, best-model forecast with CI band, and rolling 12-month average.
Exported a multi-page PDF visualization report and a self-contained interactive HTML dashboard using Chart.js.
Built an interactive 4-model comparison dashboard rendered inline in Claude with live model switching and projection table.
notes: Monthly data was synthetically interpolated from annual data using linear regression since no true monthly source data was available.
ARIMA was fitted on annual data (13 points) due to limited observations; results should be interpreted with caution.
Holt-Winters with damped trend was selected as the best-performing model based on lowest MAPE on the test set.
95% confidence intervals were approximated as ±1.96 × residual standard deviation for regression models.
Prophet model is conditionally included — requires separate installation via: pip install prophet.
All outputs are saved to a unified medicaid_output/ directory including CSV, PNG, PDF, and HTML files.
The HTML report is fully self-contained (single file) and can be opened directly in any browser without a server.

plan_next_week: Integrate real monthly Medicaid expenditure data to replace interpolated series.
Add state-level analysis alongside national projections.
Implement Prophet model and include it in the model comparison pipeline.
Improve confidence interval estimation using bootstrap resampling.
Build a Streamlit dashboard for interactive exploration of projections.
